what_input_feature: A chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the magic literal 'IHDR' (0x49 0x48 0x44 0x52) appearing a SECOND time, so png_handle_IHDR is dispatched again after png_ptr->mode already has PNG_HAVE_IHDR set, forcing the (mode & PNG_HAVE_IHDR) != 0 true branch.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves the branch in 10/10 trials and accumulates avg hitcount 20 on the blocker; I2SRandReplace can splice the logged comparand 'IHDR' (the chunk-type literal compared in the chunk dispatcher) into a second chunk-type position, manufacturing a duplicate IHDR chunk so the chunk loop re-enters png_handle_IHDR. The winning lineage shows two I2S-floor dash rows at depth 3 and 4.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile blocks in 9/10 trials with avg hitcount 0 on the blocker; lacking I2SRandReplace it relies on CMP_MAP prefix-distance feedback and havoc, which cannot reliably reconstruct the exact 4-byte 'IHDR' literal in a second chunk-type slot, so png_handle_IHDR is only entered once and (mode & PNG_HAVE_IHDR) is still zero.
mechanism_attribution: I2SRandReplace (vpc-only mutator) substitutes the logged chunk-type comparand 'IHDR' directly into a candidate chunk-type field, deterministically producing the duplicate-IHDR structure that drives the blocker's true (out-of-place) branch; value_profile has no I2SRandReplace and cannot place the exact literal.

what_input_feature: A cHRM chunk (FOURCC 0x63 0x48 0x52 0x4d) must be present and reached, AND its 4-byte big-endian length field must hold a value != 32, so png_handle_cHRM takes the true side of `if (length != 32)` at pngrutil.c:1256.
why_winner_satisfies: value_profile_cmplog resolves 10/10 and hits the true side 16 times (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1256 W T=16); its winning seeds carry the literal cHRM FOURCC bytes (offset 0x35-0x38 'cHRM') and reach the length compare, and I2S substitution can place a chunk-length value that is not 32 into the 4-byte length field that this exact compare reads.
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile blocks 8/10 and never reaches the true side (DIVERGENT BRANCHES L1256 L T=0); without I2SRandReplace it only ever falls through with length==32 (the false side, T=0 F=12), so it cannot manufacture the precise length!=32 condition at this gate and stalls 20.60h.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback; I2S logs the (length, 32) compare operands and splices a non-32 length into the input bytes that feed `if (length != 32)`, deterministically flipping the gate to its true side, which value_profile's distance-bucket feedback alone cannot accomplish.

what_input_feature: A chunk whose 4-byte type field equals the ASCII literal 'pHYs' (70 48 59 73). The PNG chunk-type dispatcher only calls png_handle_pHYs when the chunk type matches 'pHYs'; without that exact FOURCC the code at pngrutil.c:2180 (if (length != 9)) is never reached, let alone resolved.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seed 2 (vpc, trial 1) carries the literal bytes 70 48 59 73 ('pHYs') at offset 0x25-0x28, so the dispatcher invokes png_handle_pHYs and the branch is exercised 103 times; W reaches line 2180 with T=15/F=6.
why_loser_doesnt: Every loser-blocking seed has 67 41 4d 41 ('gAMA') at offset 0x25-0x28 and no pHYs FOURCC, so png_handle_pHYs is never dispatched; L records T=0 F=13 at line 2180 (the branch is never reached) and the loser blocks 9/10 trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile: the chunk-type comparison against the 'pHYs' constant is logged in the CmpLogObserver, and I2SRandReplace splices that logged 4-byte constant directly into the input where the (mutated) chunk-type bytes sit, manufacturing a valid 'pHYs' FOURCC in seconds. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and must blunder the exact 4-byte token by chance, which it fails to do.

what_input_feature: An XML input whose DTD markup declarations (e.g. <!ATTLIST>/element-content name) drive a QName into xmlSplitQName3 whose very first byte equals ':' (0x3a), so name[0] == ':' holds and the true branch returns NULL.
why_winner_satisfies: The winning vpc seeds carry the full DTD scaffold (<?xml version="1.0"?> then <!DOCTYPE a SYSTEM "dtds/1...) that reaches DTD markup-decl parsing and the xmlSplitQName3 call, and vpc resolves the colon-first comparison: the blocker line L334 shows W=T:6 vs L=T:0, i.e. only the winner ever takes the true (':' leading) side.
why_loser_doesnt: The value_profile seeds carry the same DTD scaffold and reach the same call (L334 L=F:32, blocked side only), but never place a literal ':' as the first byte of the parsed name, so name[0]==':' stays false and the branch is never resolved across 8/10 blocked trials.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog's I2SRandReplace logs the byte compared at name[0]==':' (the constant ':' = 0x3a) and splices that literal byte into the matching input position, manufacturing a colon-leading QName that pure CMP_MAP Hamming-distance feedback in value_profile cannot synthesize for a single-byte equality against a constant.

what_input_feature: A structured request whose leading bytes spell a recognizable scheme/host/port and HTTP token (e.g. the 'pop3'/'9001'/'HTTP/' literals at offsets 0x06-0x2b) so the transfer reaches multi_done with result==0 and a nonzero Curl_pgrsDone rc, taking the true branch at multi.c:654.
why_winner_satisfies: vpc winning seeds carry concentrated structural literals — '/:::' run at 0x0b-0x10, '9001' at 0x17-0x1a, and the 'GHTTP/ m' token at 0x24-0x2b — that the cmplog I2S stage substituted to satisfy the scheme/header comparisons, driving execution down to the blocker with !result true and rc nonzero (winner avg hitcount on branch 1674).
why_loser_doesnt: value_profile seeds are short, '%'-padded noise (e.g. 'CdUC%%%%!%e', './/././') lacking the matching scheme/port/HTTP literals; they never reach a state where multi_done sees result==0 with a nonzero rc, so they stay on the false side (loser avg hitcount 1).
mechanism_attribution: Both fuzzers share value_profile's comparison-distance feedback; the only technique delta is vpc's I2SRandReplace, which copies the parser's expected comparison operands directly into the input, manufacturing the exact scheme/port/HTTP-token bytes that the distance feedback alone could not climb to within budget.

what_input_feature: The leading bytes (offset 0x06 onward) must spell a recognizable URL scheme followed by '://127.0.0.1:9001/850' so curl parses a valid scheme and host:port, letting an FTP-family handler set data->set.wildcard_enabled (and thus data->state.wildcardmatch). The winner needs structured ASCII scheme/host literals, not random punctuation soup.
why_winner_satisfies: Winner-resolving seeds carry intact scheme+authority literals: 'pop8://127' / 'MQTT://127' at offsets 0x06-0x18 and '.0.0.1:9001/850' continuing, with the byte-diff showing W concentrated on '/'(0x2f) at 0x0c, '127'/'.0.0.1' digits and ':' at 0x10-0x1b. This parses as a real URL, reaching the wildcard-enabled true branch.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser-blocking seeds are degenerate runs of '%','.','-','+' punctuation (e.g. '%%%%!%e&%e$%dErF') with no scheme delimiter or host:port, so the URL parse never yields a wildcard-capable handler and wildcardmatch stays false (false branch taken 10/10).
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP. I2S substitution copies the exact comparison operands (scheme/host literal bytes curl's parser checks) directly into the input at the right offsets, synthesizing the well-formed scheme://host:port/path string that the CMP_MAP Hamming-gradient alone (value_profile) could not assemble within budget.

what_input_feature: A well-formed scheme/URL header in the leading bytes (e.g. literal 'pop3' at offset 0x06 followed by a '/' path) that drives curl's URL parser to set data->state.wildcardmatch=TRUE, after which the true side of 'if(data->state.wildcardmatch && !(PROTOPT_WILDCARD))' is taken.
why_winner_satisfies: All 5 winner seeds carry an intact protocol-string prefix: offsets 0x06-0x09 are concentrated on 70(p) 6f(o) 70(p) 33(3) ('pop3') and offsets 0x0a-0x1a hold a coherent host:port path '/?27.0.0.1:9001'; the per-role overlay shows only the winner taking the assignment line 4273 and the divergent-branch table shows the blocker T=6 F=0 for W.
why_loser_doesnt: Loser seeds are short, degenerate noise ('%' runs, repeated punctuation) with no recognizable scheme at offsets 0x06-0x09 (e.g. 64(d) 55(U) 43(C) at those offsets), so the parser never sets wildcardmatch and the blocker resolves T=0 F=10 for L.
mechanism_attribution: value_profile_cmplog adds I2SRandReplace on top of value_profile's CMP_MAP feedback; the I2S stage substitutes intercepted comparison operands (scheme tokens like 'pop3') directly into the input at the compared offsets, synthesizing the structured prefix the blocker depends on. value_profile lacks I2SRandReplace and only gets coarse Hamming/prefix-distance bucket credit, which is insufficient to assemble the multi-byte scheme literal.
